project.name: tayf
project.summary: Tayf is a Turkish news media bias analysis platform that aggregates RSS feeds from 144 Turkish news outlets, clusters related articles together, and surfaces media bias distribution, blindspots (stories covered by only one political side), and cross-spectrum surprises. It maps each source to a political zone (iktidar/bagimsiz/muhalefet) and provides visual bias spectrum breakdowns, factuality ratings, and ownership lineage for each source.

architecture.pattern: Next.js App Router with Server Components, API routes for admin/cron/health, Supabase as the database layer, and an external RSS worker for continuous ingestion. Uses Cache Components (use cache directive) for data fetching with tag-based invalidation.

keyModules.purpose: Fetches, filters, deduplicates, wire-collapses, and importance-ranks politics clusters for the homepage
Fetches a single cluster with its member articles and all sources for the detail page
Single source of truth for all bias labels, colors, spectrum ordering, and zone mapping (10 bias categories to 3 Medya DNA zones)
Detects cross-spectrum surprise outlets in a cluster (e.g., opposition outlet covering an iktidar-dominant story)
Fetches RSS feeds from all active sources in parallel with per-source header overrides
Normalizes raw RSS items into articles with category classification, image extraction, and content hashing
Hand-tagged factuality ratings and ownership metadata for ~30 major Turkish outlets
Core type definitions for the entire app: BiasCategory, MediaDnaZone, Source, Article, Cluster, NewsCategory
Main presentational card component for rendering a cluster with hero image, bias spectrum, and member article previews

architecture.dataFlow: RSS feeds are ingested by an external tmux worker (primary) or the /api/cron/ingest endpoint (fallback), normalized and upserted into Supabase's articles table. A separate cluster-worker groups related articles into clusters. The frontend fetches clusters via PostgREST embedded selects, filters for politics, applies wire-collapse/source-fairness/importance-ranking, and renders through Server Components with 30s cache TTL.

production.next: Next.js 16 framework with App Router, Cache Components, and PPR
production.@supabase/supabase-js: Supabase client for PostgREST queries and database operations
production.rss-parser: RSS/Atom feed parser with custom field support for media extensions
production.crypto-js: SHA256 hashing for article content_hash generation
production.lucide-react: Icon library used throughout the UI
production.@base-ui/react: Headless UI primitives for Button, Dialog, Select, Separator, Input components
production.class-variance-authority: Variant-based component styling for badge and button variants
production.tailwind-merge: Tailwind CSS class deduplication and merging

conventions.config: next.config.ts at root, vitest.config.ts at root, Tailwind configured inline via @theme in globals.css
conventions.testing: Vitest with Node environment, tests colocated as *.test.ts files (e.g. src/lib/bias/cross-spectrum.test.ts), @ path alias configured
